question: What is the date of the Indemnification Agreement between GreenTech Inc. and Emily J. Miller?
answer_paragraph: This Indemnification Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into on February 10, 2023, by and between GreenTech Inc., a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, New York, NY 10001 ("GreenTech"), and Emily J. Miller, residing at 456 Elm Street, San Francisco, CA 94105 ("Miller").

question: What is the coverage limit of the D&O Policy as of February 1, 2023?
answer_paragraph: On January 20, 2023, GreenTech's Board approved an increase in the coverage limit of the D&O Policy to $15 million, effective as of February 1, 2023.

question: How much has GreenTech paid in settlement to a former employee?
answer_paragraph: On November 1, 2022, GreenTech entered into a settlement agreement with a former employee, which provided for the payment of $200,000 in exchange for the release of all claims against GreenTech and its directors and officers.

question: What is the balance of the separate fund established by GreenTech to cover losses exceeding the D&O Policy coverage limit?
answer_paragraph: GreenTech has also established a separate fund to cover any losses that may exceed the coverage limit of the D&O Policy. The fund has a balance of $2 million as of the date of this Agreement.

question: What is the term of the Indemnification Agreement?
answer: until Miller ceases to be a director of GreenTech
answer_paragraph: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date first above written and shall continue until Miller ceases to be a director of GreenTech.

question: Who is the Chief Executive Officer of GreenTech Inc.?
answer_paragraph: GREENTECH INC. By: ________________________________ Name: John D. Smith Title: Chief Executive Officer

question: What is the total amount of losses incurred by GreenTech's directors and officers under the D&O Policy as of the date of the Agreement?
answer_paragraph: As of the date of this Agreement, the total amount of losses incurred by GreenTech's directors and officers under the D&O Policy is $500,000.

question: What is the deductible of the D&O Policy?
answer_paragraph: The D&O Policy has a coverage limit of $10 million and a deductible of $250,000.

question: When did GreenTech enter into a similar indemnification agreement with David L. Brown?
answer_paragraph: On August 15, 2022, GreenTech entered into a similar indemnification agreement with another director, David L. Brown.

question: When did GreenTech's Board approve the settlement agreement with a former employee?
answer_paragraph: The settlement agreement was approved by GreenTech's Board on October 25, 2022.
